channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: AWS and FlexiSAF's AI Platform Transforms Edtech in Nigeria with 130,000 Students
item.description: FlexiSAF, in partnership with AWS, has launched Distinction.app, an AI-powered edtech platform transforming education in Nigeria. The platform, built on AWS Generative AI models, offers personalized l...

item.title: Indian Navy commissions advanced stealth frigate Mahendragiri, enhancing maritime combat capabilities
item.description: The Indian Navy is set to commission its sixth indigenous Project 17A stealth frigate, Mahendragiri (F38), at Visakhapatnam on July 11, 2026. Designed by the Navy’s Warship Design Bureau and built by ...

item.title: Analysis of senator-judges' votes in Sara Duterte's impeachment trial reveals legislative coalitions
item.description: The article discusses the procedural votes by senator-judges during Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment trial, highlighting how these intermediate decisions reflect underlying political preferen...

item.title: SARS Introduces Enhanced Digital Tools to Improve Tax Compliance and Security
item.description: The South African Revenue Service (SARS) has launched significant digital enhancements to streamline the tax return process, making it simpler, faster, and more secure for taxpayers. Over 1.9 million ...

item.title: Supreme Court Backs Trump's Immigration Policies Despite Birthright Citizenship Concerns
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court has endorsed significant aspects of former President Donald Trump's immigration policies, including the controversial 'birthright citizenship' debate, despite ongoing legal and ...

item.title: Tanzania and China Discuss Visa-Free Transit for Eligible Travelers
item.description: Tanzania and China are currently in discussions to introduce a pilot visa-free transit arrangement, allowing eligible Tanzanians traveling through China to third countries to enter the Asian nation wi...

item.title: Sara Duterte's Impeachment Trial: Key Developments and Related News
item.description: The article highlights key developments in Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment trial, including Senator Chiz Escudero setting the conviction threshold at 16 votes. It details the trial's progres...

item.title: Large Hadron Collider Retires After 18 Years, Upgrades Begin
item.description: The Large Hadron Collider (LHC), a monumental scientific instrument, has officially entered its third long shutdown after 18 years of groundbreaking research. This retirement marks the end of an era f...

item.title: Police Investigate Death of Advocate in Athi River
item.description: A 30-year-old advocate, Edward Kariuki Muthee, was found dead outside a residential estate in Athi River, Machakos County. Police confirmed his body showed signs of blunt force trauma to the head and ...

item.title: Coyote Attack on Toddler at Whitby Playground: Police Report Unprovoked Incident
item.description: A two-year-old boy was bitten in the face by a coyote during an unprovoked attack at a playground in Whitby, Ontario, on Sunday evening. Durham Region police confirmed that officers responded to the s...

item.title: Police Inquiry Stakes and the Path to Safer South Africa
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing impact of the Madlanga commission of inquiry, established a year after KwaZulu-Natal police commissioner Lt-Gen Nhlanhla Mkhwanazi's explosive allegations of politica...

item.title: Home Sodium Batteries for Energy Storage: A New Approach to Managing Electricity Costs
item.description: The article explores the potential of domestic sodium batteries as a solution for optimizing electricity costs in residential settings. It explains how these batteries could be used to store energy du...

item.title: Eight arrested in ₹12 crore sugar factory fraud case
item.description: The District Police in Vijayapura have arrested eight individuals involved in a prolonged fraud scheme at a sugar factory, defrauding farmers and the factory itself of ₹12 crore over six years. The in...

item.title: Ethiopia's IMF funds arrive as inflation challenges central bank's control
item.description: Ethiopia has received a $464 million disbursement from the International Monetary Fund (IMF), marking the completion of the country's fifth review. This financial support is critical as the National B...

item.title: Concerns Rise Over Mitch McConnell's Health and Elaine Chao's Unusual Diplomatic Activities
item.description: Prominent MAGA activist Kylie Jane Kremer has escalated concerns over Senator Mitch McConnell (R-KY) following his recent hospitalization after being found unconscious. Kremer, associated with Women f...

item.title: Pakistan Army Committed to Ensuring Water Rights Amid India Disputes
item.description: The Pakistan Army has reaffirmed its commitment to securing the country's rightful share of water amid ongoing tensions with India, as highlighted in a statement released by the Inter-Services Public ...

item.title: FIFA President Reaffirms Judicial Independence Amid Trump's Inquiry on Balogun Suspension
item.description: FIFA President Gianni Infantino has confirmed that U.S. President Donald Trump contacted him regarding the one-match suspension of USA forward Folarin Balogun. However, Infantino emphasized that the c...

item.title: Climate Change Disrupts Bird Migration Patterns, Threatening Populations
item.description: Climate change is causing significant disruptions to bird migration patterns, with far-reaching consequences for ecosystems and biodiversity. A study by Professor Morgan Tingley of the University of C...

item.title: Canada's Services Economy Contracts in June Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty and Inflation Concerns
item.description: Canada's services economy contracted in June, marking its lowest level since February, according to S&P Global's PMI data. The Business Activity Index dropped to 47.1 from 50.6 in May, indicating a co...

item.title: Student Unrest Forces Closure of Kisii School Over New Principal Appointment
item.description: Nyabisase Mixed Senior School in Kisii County was forced to close after violent clashes erupted between students and teachers following the appointment of a new principal. The Teachers Service Commiss...
